results.group_hx5kb98/Q9_Final_question_our_surv: The project is very unique in it's kind working on new activities like Index based Livestock Insurance, Loan provision, Livestock and Livestock product marketing,  which was not largely practiced before and we have started in a large scale, as a new activity the straggle to raise community awareness on these activities were very challenging. The problem related with Livestock marketing and pastoral community largely related with saving their animals from recurrent drought occurrence in the project intervention area for which the project implement Index Based Livestock Insurance (IBLI) and introduce livestock marketing where there were no commercial livestock production   for which we are intensively focusing on strengthening existing pastoral cooperatives and establishing new cooperatives where necessary, working on quality of marketed animals was another problem related to livestock marketing for the same, so we were focusing on strengthening capacity of regional Laboratories in all possible angels, trade facilitation and trade logistics also were the focus area of the project,  Loan provision to cooperatives and private investors with the help of Ethiopian Development Bank which has no more branch at grass root level and has no sufficient experience with cooperative was and still is also another challenge , accessibility is also a challenge in this areas as the the intervention areas are spared all over the boarder of the country, we are working in 187 woredas in the area covers more than 60% the country's total area coverage.

The procurement procedures and approval processes took approximately one year, which significantly affected the project's schedule performance and delayed the commencement of implementation activities.
2.Securing the required Right-of-Way (ROW) corridor for pole erection has been one of the major challenges encountered during project implementation. 
3.As this is a rehabilitation project involving existing power infrastructure, obtaining planned power interruptions (outages) requested by the contractor has been particularly challenging.

1. Working with different government and non-government organizations regularly and effectively is a major challenge of the biogas program, both at the national and regional levels.
2. Once the project is completed,  regions are expected to sustain the project with limited support to make the community the best users of the technology. However, regions need the same project all the time. 
Therefore, local leaders need to be capacitated to sustain reliable and best projects which can make the community the best users after the completion of the project period. In addition, making the existing project as functional as possible. 
3. Project implementation knowledge is very important at the regional and national level. In some areas, even reporting their accomplished activities is very difficult. Therefore, local capacity building is very important for our project areas.  
4. The functionality rate of the technologies after the project period. It needs close attention to project implementation.

The significant challenges have been the application of the Project Procurement Strategy for Development (PPSD) approach and the use of Rated Criteria in bid evaluations. Limited familiarity with this approach contributed to prolonged bid evaluation periods and considerable time was required to understand and consistently apply the criteria.
Security-related restrictions have limited access to project sites for Technical team to gather the necessary data and prepare the Bid document resulting in delays in site investigations and overall implementation progress.
ROW compensation challenge is the limited awareness and understanding of the new proclamation among key stakeholders.

results.group_hx5kb98/Q9_Final_question_our_surv: This project is the first large-scale initiative covering 23 towns across multiple regions, primarily focused on implementing sanitation infrastructure. Project performance has been affected by the limited capacity of implementing entities and the private sector in the sanitation area, resulting in long design preparation, frequent design changes and cost overruns, as well as complex environmental and social safeguard requirements involving resident resettlement; security challenges that disrupt material supply; and recent fuel shortages, all of which have contributed to implementation delays.
